binaries.title: mrxsmb.sys - Read-after-free of VNetRoot device-object pointer in tree-connect completion (SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction @ 0x1C0019DB8), feature-gated fix

changed_functions.name: SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction
key_changes: PATCHED: Pre-captures *(arg1+0x18) (device object pointer, pMRxDeviceObject) into a stack slot at function entry (0x1C0019DF6) BEFORE SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext can free the VNetRoot context, avoiding a read of a potentially freed object at the merge point

PATCHED: At the RxPostToWorkerThread call site, uses the cached value (0x1C001A0DD) instead of re-reading *(arg1+0x18) from the VNetRoot context that may have been freed by SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext

PATCHED: The capture and its consumption are gated by the WIL feature flag Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (called at 0x1C0019DED and 0x1C001A0CA). When the feature is DISABLED, the original live read from *(arg1+0x18) is retained at 0x1C001A0F2 (staged rollout, old path retained)

UNPATCHED: Error path calls SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rsi) at 0x1C0019FB3 which may free the VNetRoot context, then reads *(rsi+0x18) at 0x1C0019F2D from the potentially freed object (read-after-free, CWE-416)

Non-security: Register allocation reorganized (rbp pushed instead of spilled; status held in r14 instead of rbp); basic-block count grew from 50 to 55 for the gated caching logic. Newly added helpers Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage and Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledFallback are WIL feature-staging boilerplate
changed_functions.security_notes: READ-AFTER-FREE (CWE-416): SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction (0x1C0019DB8) is the completion handler for an SMB tree-connect (VNetRoot construction) exchange. When the NTSTATUS argument (arg3) is negative, the error path calls SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext then SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rsi) at 0x1C0019FB3, which decrements the VNetRoot context reference count and, on the last reference, tears down and frees the object. Execution then jumps to the merge point at 0x1C0019F03 (shared with the success path), where 0x1C0019F2D executes 'mov rcx, [rsi+18h]' — reading the pMRxDeviceObject pointer from the potentially freed context — and passes it to RxPostToWorkerThread at 0x1C0019F31. The patched build pre-reads [rdi+18h] into a stack slot at entry (0x1C0019DF6) before any dereference and uses that cached copy at the merge point. The capture is gated by Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage(); when the feature is disabled the original live read is retained at 0x1C001A0F2, so the mitigation is a staged rollout, not an unconditional fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED error path -> read-after-free ===
0x1C0019F98: and     qword ptr [r14+28h], 0     ; clear context pointer
0x1C0019FA0: and     qword ptr [rsi+1E0h], 0    ; clear VNetRoot back-pointer
0x1C0019FA8: mov     rcx, rsi
0x1C0019FAB: call    SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext
0x1C0019FB0: mov     rcx, rsi                   ; rsi = VNetRoot context (arg1)
0x1C0019FB3: call    SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext  ; may free rsi on last reference
0x1C0019FB8: jmp     loc_1C0019F03              ; to merge point

; Merge point (BOTH success and error paths):
0x1C0019F03: mov     r9, [r15+110h]
0x1C0019F0A: lea     r8, [r15+0C8h]
0x1C0019F11: neg     r13b
0x1C0019F14: mov     [r15+118h], ebp
0x1C0019F1B: mov     [rsp+58h+pContext], r15
0x1C0019F20: sbb     eax, eax
0x1C0019F22: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0019F24: and     eax, ebp
0x1C0019F26: mov     [r15+11Ch], eax
0x1C0019F2D: mov     rcx, [rsi+18h]             ; read pMRxDeviceObject (read-after-free on error path)
0x1C0019F31: call    cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

=== PATCHED: capture at entry, gated consumption ===
0x1C0019DD3: and     [rsp+68h+arg_8], 0         ; init cached ptr to NULL
0x1C0019DED: call    Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0019DF2: test    eax, eax
0x1C0019DF4: jz      short loc_1C0019DFF        ; skip capture if feature disabled
0x1C0019DF6: mov     rax, [rdi+18h]            ; capture *(arg1+0x18) BEFORE any deref
0x1C0019DFA: mov     [rsp+68h+arg_8], rax

; error path deref (same structure as unpatched):
0x1C001A0A8: call    SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext
0x1C001A0B0: call    SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext  ; may free rdi

; merge point (patched):
0x1C001A0CA: call    Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C001A0CF: mov     r9, [rsi+110h]
0x1C001A0D6: lea     r8, [rsi+0C8h]
0x1C001A0DD: mov     rcx, [rsp+68h+arg_8]      ; use cached ptr
0x1C001A0E2: xor     edx, edx
0x1C001A0E4: test    eax, eax
0x1C001A0E6: mov     [rsp+68h+pContext], rsi
0x1C001A0EB: setnz   dl
0x1C001A0EE: test    edx, edx
0x1C001A0F0: jnz     short loc_1C001A0F6       ; if feature enabled, keep cached ptr
0x1C001A0F2: mov     rcx, [rdi+18h]           ; retained live read when feature disabled
0x1C001A0F8: call    cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

security_findings.entry_point: MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot (RDBSS mini-redirector callback at 0x1C0017090) — reached by UNC path access (NtCreateFile("\\server\share"), WNetAddConnection, CreateFile on a network path)
call_chain: User-mode: NtCreateFile/WNetAddConnection on a UNC path (\\server\share)
MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot initiates an SMB tree-connect exchange
On asynchronous exchange completion, Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize (0x1C0019D90) calls SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction(arg1=[rcx+50h], arg2=[rcx+1B8h], arg3=[rcx+8]=NTSTATUS)
SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction: if NTSTATUS < 0 -> SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rsi) at 0x1C0019FB3 (may free the context)
SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction: read *(rsi+0x18) at 0x1C0019F2D -> RxPostToWorkerThread with a pointer from the potentially freed context
Note: the synchronous call from MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot at 0x1C00174EF passes status=0 (success) and does NOT take the error/deref path
security_findings.description: SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction (0x1C0019DB8) is the completion handler for an SMB tree-connect (VNetRoot construction) exchange in mrxsmb.sys. The third argument (arg3) is the NTSTATUS from the exchange. When the status is negative (e.g. server unreachable, access denied, bad network name, timeout, or when the function sets 0xC00000CB for a NetRoot type byte at [VNetRoot+0xBC] not equal to 0/1/3, or 0xC0410001 for a caching-mode condition), the error path is taken. The error path calls SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext then SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rsi) at 0x1C0019FB3, which decrements the VNetRoot context reference count and, on the last reference, tears down and frees the object. After this returns, the error path jumps to the merge point at 0x1C0019F03 (shared with the success path), where instruction 0x1C0019F2D executes 'mov rcx, [rsi+18h]', reading the pMRxDeviceObject pointer from the VNetRoot context that may have just been freed, and passes it as the first argument to RxPostToWorkerThread at 0x1C0019F31. The patched build captures *(arg1+0x18) into a stack slot at function entry (0x1C0019DF6) before any dereference, gated by Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage(), and uses the cached copy at the merge point; the original live read is retained at 0x1C001A0F2 when the feature is disabled.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Triggered when a VNetRoot (tree-connect) construction exchange completes with a negative NTSTATUS via the asynchronous completion path Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize (0x1C0019D90). Conditions: (A) the exchange NTSTATUS (arg3) is negative — unreachable server, STATUS_NETWORK_ACCESS_DENIED (0xC00000CB), STATUS_BAD_NETWORK_NAME (0xC00000CC), or timeout; (B) the NetRoot type byte at [VNetRoot+0xBC] is not 0/1/3, which the function converts to status 0xC00000CB; (C) a caching-mode condition that sets status 0xC0410001. Simplest trigger: a local user accesses a UNC path to a share that the SMB server rejects. For the read to actually be a use-after-free, SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext must drop the LAST reference to the VNetRoot context; if other references remain, the object survives and the read is safe.
security_findings.impact: The read at 0x1C0019F2D returns a pointer field (pMRxDeviceObject) from a possibly-freed VNetRoot context; that pointer is passed to RxPostToWorkerThread as the device-object argument. If the object was freed and the stale pointer is invalid, dereferencing it in the worker path can crash the system (e.g. bugcheck 0x50 PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA). No exploit primitive beyond a stale kernel-pointer read/dereference is demonstrable from these two builds; there is no evidence in the disassembly for controlled reclamation, function-pointer hijack, or code execution, and none is asserted. Severity is assessed as Medium: reachability depends on the reference-count state at error completion, and the delivered fix is feature-gated with the unsafe live read retained in the else-branch.
security_findings.assembly:
; === UNPATCHED SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction (mrxsmb_unpatched.sys) — key flow ===
0x1C0019DB8: mov     [rsp+arg_8], rbx
0x1C0019DC7: push    rdi ... push r15
0x1C0019DD4: mov     rdi, [rcx+1A8h]            ; NetRoot entry
0x1C0019DE2: mov     r15, rdx                   ; r15 = exchange context (arg2)
0x1C0019DEC: mov     ebp, r8d                    ; ebp = NTSTATUS (arg3)
0x1C0019DF4: mov     rsi, rcx                    ; rsi = arg1 = VNetRoot context
0x1C0019E08: test    r8d, r8d
0x1C0019E0B: js      loc_1C0019EB6              ; if status < 0 -> error path
; ... error path:
0x1C0019F98: and     qword ptr [r14+28h], 0
0x1C0019FA0: and     qword ptr [rsi+1E0h], 0
0x1C0019FA8: mov     rcx, rsi
0x1C0019FAB: call    SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext
0x1C0019FB0: mov     rcx, rsi
0x1C0019FB3: call    SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext  ; may free rsi
0x1C0019FB8: jmp     loc_1C0019F03
; merge point:
0x1C0019F03: mov     r9, [r15+110h]
0x1C0019F0A: lea     r8, [r15+0C8h]
0x1C0019F14: mov     [r15+118h], ebp
0x1C0019F1B: mov     [rsp+58h+pContext], r15
0x1C0019F22: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0019F26: mov     [r15+11Ch], eax
0x1C0019F2D: mov     rcx, [rsi+18h]             ; pMRxDeviceObject (read-after-free on error path)
0x1C0019F31: call    cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread
0x1C0019F59: retn

; === Async completion caller ===
0x1C0019D90: Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize:
0x1C0019D94:   mov r8d, [rcx+8]     ; arg3 = NTSTATUS
0x1C0019D98:   mov rdx, [rcx+1B8h]  ; arg2 = exchange context
0x1C0019D9F:   mov rcx, [rcx+50h]   ; arg1 = VNetRoot context
0x1C0019DA3:   call SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction

breakpoints.why: Break at entry to inspect arg1 (rcx = VNetRoot context), arg2 (rdx = exchange context), arg3 (r8d = NTSTATUS). Confirm r8d is negative so the error path will be taken. Note [rcx+0x18], the pointer that will be re-read at the merge point.
At entry rcx = rsi = VNetRoot context. Run !pool @rcx before stepping over, then re-check @rsi after return to see if the pool block was freed.
The exact read instruction. After the error path, rsi may point to a freed VNetRoot context. Run !pool @rsi to confirm whether the block is freed/reallocated.
Break to confirm the call chain from RDBSS. Note the synchronous call to SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction at 0x1C00174EF passes status=0 and does not take the error path.

watch: rcx at 0x1C0019DB8 = arg1 = VNetRoot context pointer (the object that may be freed)
r8d at 0x1C0019DB8 = arg3 = NTSTATUS (must be negative to take the error path; e.g. 0xC00000CB)
rsi throughout the unpatched function = VNetRoot context (same as arg1/rcx at entry); watch [rsi+0x18]
[rsi+8] region = VNetRoot reference count (SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext decrements it; last reference frees the object)
rcx at 0x1C0019F2D = value read from [rsi+0x18], passed to RxPostToWorkerThread
key_offsets: 0x1C0019EBF: 'js loc_1C0019F74' — branch sending negative-status completions to the error path
0x1C0019FB3: 'call SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext' — dereference that may free the context
0x1C0019F2D: 'mov rcx, qword [rsi+0x18]' — the read from the possibly-freed context (patched build uses the cached [rsp] slot instead when the feature is enabled)
0x1C0019D90: 'Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize' — async completion caller that passes the exchange NTSTATUS
0x1C00174EF: 'call SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction' inside MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot — synchronous call with status=0 (does NOT take the error path)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1) Stand up an SMB server that completes negotiation but returns an error in the TREE_CONNECT response (non-existent share, or an access-denied/bad-network-name status). 2) From the target (unpatched mrxsmb.sys) access a UNC path to the server, e.g. `net use \\attacker_ip\badshare`. 3) The negative NTSTATUS flows through Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize into SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction with a negative arg3 and takes the error path. 4) Inspect the pool state at 0x1C0019F2D to determine whether the VNetRoot context was freed by the preceding dereference.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the read breakpoint (0x1C0019F2D): if the dereference dropped the last reference, !pool @rsi shows the VNetRoot context block freed or reallocated, and the value read from [rsi+0x18] is stale. Dereferencing that stale device-object pointer in the worker path can crash the system (e.g. bugcheck 0x50 PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA). If other references to the context remain, the object survives and the read returns valid data.
debugging_aids.notes: The VNetRoot context pool tag/size can be read via !pool @rcx at 0x1C0019DB8 before the dereference. Whether the object is actually freed depends on the reference count at [VNetRoot+8]; other outstanding references keep it alive and turn this into a latent bug. The fix is gated by the WIL feature flag Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage; when the feature is disabled the patched build retains the live read at 0x1C001A0F2, so the mitigation is a staged rollout and may not be active in all configurations.

summary: The patch adds a feature-gated hardening in mrxsmb.sys (SMB redirector) for a read-after-free (CWE-416) in SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction (0x1C0019DB8), the completion handler for SMB tree-connect (VNetRoot construction). When the exchange completes with a negative NTSTATUS via Smb2ConstructVNetRoot_Finalize (0x1C0019D90), the error path calls SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rsi) at 0x1C0019FB3 (which may free the VNetRoot context on the last reference); all paths then merge and read *(VNetRoot+0x18) at 0x1C0019F2D, passing that pointer to RxPostToWorkerThread. The patched build pre-captures *(arg1+0x18) into a stack slot at function entry (0x1C0019DF6) before any dereference, gated by the WIL feature flag Feature_1585957178__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage(); the original live read is retained at 0x1C001A0F2 when the feature is disabled. Independent diff confirms only this function changed logic (plus the added WIL feature-flag helpers); all other differences are WPP tracing and register renaming. Severity Medium: exploitability depends on the reference-count state at error completion, no primitive beyond a stale-pointer read/dereference is demonstrable, and the fix is a staged (feature-gated) rollout with the unsafe path retained.
